summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a magical 1984 Berkeley show, highlighted by an epic Dark Star and scorching Scarlet > Touch > Fire sequence.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear soundboard quality that lets every instrument shine, especially Bob’s whammy‑bar guitar work. Musically the night is legendary – Jerry finally “shows up” in ’84, Dark Star stretches out for over 16 minutes and the Scarlet > Touch > Fire combo burns hot alongside standout renditions of Stella Blue, Sugar Magnolia and The Greatest Story Ever Told.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance (epic Dark Star, rare Jerry presence) and recording quality (crisp soundboard), with no substantive negatives across many detailed reviews.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers describe the audio as crisp, beautiful and beautifully recorded; no drop‑outs or balance issues mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans rave about the seamless flow into a long, hypnotic second set with Dark Star as the centerpiece; the night is described as magical and perfectly aligned.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His rare 1984 appearance was noted as a highlight; his guitar work described as raging on C.C. Rider and delivering great tone throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned for guiding the crowd into Dark Star and keeping the vibe relaxed, but no specific bass commentary.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for an audible, precise whammy‑bar style and a standout jam on The Greatest Story Ever Told.
